title: Cosmos Minesweeper — clear the field, flag the mines
summary: A real, self-contained Minesweeper game in one HTML file with zero dependencies. Play on a 9x9 grid hiding 10 mines: left-click (or tap) reveals a cell, right-click (or long-press) plants a flag, numbers show how many mines touch a cell, and revealing a zero floods open its whole empty region. A live mine counter and timer sit in the HUD, the first click is always safe, hitting a mine ends the round, and clearing every safe cell wins. R, the smiley, or the button restart. It ships with a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a logical solver opens a seeded board cell-by-cell, flagging obvious mines, as a pure function of time.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: left-click/tap a cell to reveal it, right-click or long-press to flag/unflag a suspected mine; the first reveal is guaranteed safe. GOAL: reveal every cell that is not a mine without detonating one — numbers count adjacent mines, revealing a 0 flood-opens its empty neighbourhood, the MINES counter shows mines minus flags placed and the TIME counter ticks up; clear all safe cells to win, hit a mine to lose; press R, click the smiley, or Restart to play again. KEY TECHNIQUES: flood-fill reveal on zero cells and a logical solver that applies the two classic deduction rules (flags==count -> open the rest; hidden+flags==count -> flag the rest); the AUTO attract demo is fully deterministic — mulberry32 seeds a fixed board with a guaranteed-safe zero start, solveScript precomputes the ordered open/flag actions, and autoState(ms) replays them as a pure function of time so window.__seek(t) reproduces the exact revealed numbers, flags and mine counter from t alone. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: STEP_MS per-action pace, HOLD_MS finished-board hold, T0OFF (frame-0 offset), MINES count, seed search in pickSolve.
